citations.narrative:
***The left wall in the gray fox enclosure has a layer of wire that has separated from the wall and is hanging into the
enclosure. The wire may allow the fox to become injured or entangled in it.
The wire shall be tacked up, repaired or removed to protect the fox from injury.
Correct by May 6, 2026
***The fence dividing the goat and sheep enclosure has a portion that has been rolled back, said to allow access between
the pens. The fence material is partially laying on the ground with the upper portion still attached to the fence. The
animals in the enclosure have access to this area and may come in contact with this fence possibly becoming entangled,
trapped or injured.
The fence shall be repaired and if the area is to be used to allow passage between the enclosures it shall be constructed
in a manner that will protect the animals from harm.
Correct by May 6, 2026.

***There is several days accumulation of food that has been left laying on the ground along the fence line in the hog pen.
Fresh food is being provided without removing food waste from previous days feeding creating an accumulation that
allows contamination of daily feed. The accumulations attract insects and vermin which increases the risk of disease
hazard and illness.
The waste material shall be removed from the pen regularly to prevent accumulations and contamination thereby
minimizing the risk of disease or illness.
Correct by May 2, 2026.

citations.desc: Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being.
citations.narrative:
***The salmon crested cockatoo "Alex" is plucking his feathers. The bird is known to exhibit this behavior when moved to
different enclosures and it appears when housed beside another cockatoo. This behavior has not been addressed in the
bird enrichment plan nor has any plan been put in place to attempt to alleviate the cause of the feather plucking.
A plan shall be developed to attempt to alleviate the cause of the abnormal behavior and it shall be documented in the
bird enrichment plan.
Correct by May 13, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Licensee.
n

narrative:
3.125(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
***The left wall in the gray fox enclosure has a layer of wire that has separated from the wall and is hanging into the 
enclosure. The wire may allow the fox to become injured or entangled in it. 
 
The wire shall be tacked up, repaired or removed to protect the fox from injury. 
 
Correct by May 6, 2026 
 
***The fence dividing the goat and sheep enclosure has a portion that has been rolled back, said to allow access between 
the pens. The fence material is partially laying on the ground with the upper portion still attached to the fence. The 
animals in the enclosure have access to this area and may come in contact with this fence possibly becoming entangled, 
trapped or injured.  
 
The fence shall be repaired and if the area is to be used to allow passage between the enclosures it shall be constructed 
in a manner that will protect the animals from harm. 
 
Correct by May 6, 2026. 
3.131(a) 
   
Sanitation. 
***There is several days accumulation of food that has been left laying on the ground along the fence line in the hog pen. 
Fresh food is being provided without removing food waste from previous days feeding creating an accumulation that 
allows contamination of daily feed. The accumulations attract insects and vermin which increases the risk of disease 
hazard and illness. 
 
The waste material shall be removed from the pen regularly to prevent accumulations and contamination thereby 
minimizing the risk of disease or illness.

Correct by May 2, 2026. 
3.154(c)(2) 
   
Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being. 
***The salmon crested cockatoo "Alex" is plucking his feathers. The bird is known to exhibit this behavior when moved to 
different enclosures and it appears when housed beside another cockatoo. This behavior has not been addressed in the 
bird enrichment plan nor has any plan been put in place to attempt to alleviate the cause of the feather plucking. 
 
A plan shall be developed to attempt to alleviate the cause of the abnormal behavior and it shall be documented in the 
bird enrichment plan. 
 
Correct by May 13, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the Licensee.
